'''Adam Maher''' (2 August 1972 – 25 February 2020) was an Australian professional [[rugby league]] player. Between 1994 and 1996, he played 43 games for the [[Cronulla Sharks]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2020/02/27/vale-adam-maher-sharks-mourn-forwards-death-from-motor-neurone-disease/|title=Vale, Adam Maher: Sharks mourn forward's death from motor neurone disease|publisher=[[NRL]]|date=27 February 2020|accessdate=27 February 2020}}</ref> Four years later, Maher started a playing career in the United Kingdom, representing [[Rochdale Hornets]] (1998), [[Gateshead Thunder (1999)|Gateshead Thunder]] (1999) and most notably [[Hull FC]] (2000–03).<ref name=RLP>{{Cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/adam-maher/summary.html|title=Adam Maher|publisher=Rugby League Project|accessdate=27 February 2020}}</ref> During his time at Hull, he made 100 appearances for the [[Super League]]  club, scoring 25 tries.<ref name=7news>{{Cite news|url=https://7news.com.au/sport/rugby-league/emotional-tributes-as-tough-and-kind-adam-maher-loses-battle-with-mnd-aged-47-c-718696|title=Former rugby league player Adam Maher dies aged 47 after battle with MND|work=Seven News|date=27 February 2020|accessdate=27 February 2020}}</ref>

In late 2018, Maher was diagnosed with [[ALS|motor neurone disease]]. He died on 25 February 2020, at the age of 47.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2020/02/27/vale-adam-maher-sharks-mourn-forwards-death-from-motor-neurone-disease/|title=Vale, Adam Maher: Sharks mourn forward's death from motor neurone disease|publisher=[[NRL]]|date=27 February 2020|accessdate=27 February 2020}}</ref>
